steps.title: Identify the property at issue
steps.content: The passage states that silver exhibits “high electrical conductivity” immediately before the blank. This is the one stated fact that will drive the researchers' hypothesis.

steps.title: Connect the property to the result
steps.content:
Given that silver nanoparticles were added and power output more than doubled, the researchers would logically hypothesize that silver's conductivity provides a solution to the identified problem: the inefficient redox reactions.

Choice D states this directly: silver nanoparticles may allow electrons to bypass the series of redox reactions and transfer directly to the electrode.
  • A incorrectly focuses on bacterial metabolism rather than electron transfer efficiency
  • B contradicts the experiment by implying silver's addition is irrelevant
  • C reverses the logic by suggesting silver catalyzes the problematic reactions

That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
